evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG interface / TAP requester
evidence_slots.object: DMI/debug module request interface and dm_top debug capability
evidence_slots.expected_guard: DMI/debug requests should be enabled only after explicit JTAG authentication/unlock such as pass_check/pass_mode/hashValid/jtag_unlock; unauthenticated TAP shifting alone must not assert valid DMI operations.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: The dmi_jtag state machine accepts TAP DMIACCESS/update_dr and enters Read when the DMI op is read and `(pass_check | ~we_flag == 1)`. pass_check is defaulted low in combinational logic and asserted only in the password-check-valid state, while jtag_unlock_o is assigned directly to pass_check. In Read and Write states, dmi_req_valid is asserted and forwarded through dmi_cdc to dmi_req_valid_o/debug_req_valid. Thus when we_flag_4 is low, DMI reads can be generated without a completed authentication comparison or persistent unlock.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG pins tck/tms/trst/td_i -> dmi_jtag_tap DMIACCESS/update_dr -> dmi_jtag state machine -> dmi_cdc -> riscv_peripherals debug_req/debug_resp -> dm_top DMI interface
evidence_slots.state_condition: dmi_jtag reset sets state_q Idle, dr_q/address_q/data_q zero, error_q DMINoError; no persistent resettable authenticated state was observed for pass_check/jtag_unlock. dmi_reset clears only the DMI error state. test_logic_reset clears DR. testmode_i affects TAP TDO clock mux only in observed code.
evidence_slots.impact: Unauthenticated external JTAG may issue DMI reads and reach debug-module registers/capabilities when we_flag_4 is deasserted.

rtl_evidence.detail: dmi_req fields assigned from address_q/data_q; jtag_unlock_o assigned to pass_check.
Defaults include dmi_req_valid=0 and pass_check=0; Idle transitions allow Read when read op and `(pass_check | ~we_flag == 1)`, while Write requires pass_check.
Read and Write states assert dmi_req_valid.
pass_check asserted only when hashValid and exp_hash equals pass_hash.
dmi_cdc forwards dmi_req_valid to dmi_req_valid_o.
CDC forwards JTAG request valid/data to core DMI valid/data.
i_dmi_jtag connects dmi_req_valid_o to debug_req_valid and we_flag to we_flag_4.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: External JTAG can assert DMI request-valid for at least read operations without a persistent authenticated unlock state when we_flag is deasserted.
inspection_records.notes: This is a candidate violation because the observed request-valid assignment is not dominated by a persistent jtag_unlock/pass_check guard for reads.

evidence_slots.expected_guard: Fuse-stored JTAG hash or equivalent secret/authenticator should not be directly software-readable or exposed through unprivileged MMIO/debug readback; if readable, access should be restricted by privilege/lifecycle/lock/read-protection.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: fuse_mem contains constant entries for the JTAG expected HMAC hash, HMAC key hashes, key material, access-control values, and crypto keys. Its read port latches addr_i on req_i and returns mem[addr_q] without a local privilege or read-protect check. Top-level connects fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata through pkt_wrapper, which receives reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i, but pkt_wrapper internals were not provided; therefore the actual software-visible read protection for fuse address ranges remains uncertain.
evidence_slots.path: NoC packet port -> pkt AXI bridge -> pkt_wrapper fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o -> fuse_mem rdata_o; fuse_mem jtag_hash_o/ikey_hash_o/okey_hash_o -> riscv_peripherals jtag_hash/ikey_hash/okey_hash -> dmi_jtag HMAC comparison
evidence_slots.state_condition: fuse_mem contents are constant modeled values; addr_q updates on req_i; rdata_o is derived from mem[addr_q].
evidence_slots.impact: If pkt_wrapper exposes arbitrary fuse reads, software could read authentication references and other fuse secrets.

addr_q is latched on req_i and rdata_o returns mem[addr_q] without local privilege/read-protection.

evidence_slots.subject: NoC/MMIO master targeting riscv_peripherals
evidence_slots.object: security-sensitive peripheral MMIO regions and control registers, especially debug module registers
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Protected peripheral accesses should be mediated by acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl and privilege/debug-mode policy consistently on both reads and writes before forwarding request/valid/write-enable to the peripheral.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Most security-sensitive wrappers receive reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i derived from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][index], and some receive debug_mode_i. In contrast, the debug NoC bridge drives dm_master AXI signals, which axi2mem converts into dm_slave_req/we/addr/wdata for dm_top; no top-level acc_ctrl/reglk/debug-mode gate was observed on this path. Boot ROM has explicit `rom_req = rom_req_acct && acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]`, showing such top-level gating exists elsewhere.
evidence_slots.path: buf_ariane_debug_noc2 -> i_debug_axilite_bridge -> dm_master AXI -> i_dm_axi2mem -> dm_slave_req/we/addr/wdata -> dm_top
evidence_slots.state_condition: acc_ctrl_c is combinationally derived and indexed by priv_lvl_i. debug_mode_i is passed to selected wrappers but not observed on the debug bridge path.
evidence_slots.impact: A NoC requester able to target the debug port may access debug-module registers without intended access-matrix or lock mediation.

rtl_evidence.detail: reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl declared and acc_ctrl_c derived.
axi2mem converts dm_master AXI into dm_slave_req/we/addr/wdata/rdata.
debug NoC AXI-lite bridge directly drives dm_master AXI signals.
Boot ROM request is explicitly gated with acc_ctrl_c, unlike the observed debug bridge.
Other wrappers receive reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i and sometimes debug_mode_i.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: The debug NoC path appears routed directly to the debug module slave interface without visible acc_ctrl/reglk/debug-mode mediation at top level.
inspection_records.notes: Wrapper internals are not closed, but the unmediated top-level debug path is sufficient for a candidate finding.

evidence_slots.expected_guard: Once a lock bit in reglk_ctrl is set, it should be sticky or only clearable by reset/authorized lifecycle transition, and it must gate every write/configuration path to the locked asset including debug/test paths.

However, the actual reglk_ctrl reset/update/stickiness semantics and byte/partial-write handling are inside wrapper logic not provided in the evidence. Several protected wrappers appear to consume reglk_ctrl_i, but complete use-site coverage cannot be proven.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible requester
evidence_slots.object: acc_ctrl and acc_ctrl_c access-control policy bits
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Access-control policy registers should be writable only by authorized privileged software or trusted lifecycle logic, and updates should not allow unprivileged subjects to grant themselves access to protected peripherals.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: acc_ctrl is driven out of acct_wrapper, which is reachable through a dedicated NoC AXI-lite bridge. acct_wrapper receives reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i derived from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][6], and we_flag_0. Top-level derives acc_ctrl_c from acc_ctrl with an alias/override: for j==5, acc_ctrl_c[i][5] is ORed with acc_ctrl[4*4+i]. The evidence shows downstream use of acc_ctrl_c for many wrappers, but acct_wrapper internal write authorization and reset defaults were not provided.

evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset/default acc_ctrl policy, write enable assignment, and race behavior with active transactions are not visible. Alias expression for peripheral 5 is visible.
evidence_slots.impact: If acct_wrapper allows insufficiently privileged writes, a requester may modify acc_ctrl to grant access to protected peripherals; aliasing could unintentionally grant peripheral 5 access based on peripheral 4 policy.

acct NoC AXI-lite bridge provides software-visible read/write channel to acct_wrapper.

evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG interface
evidence_slots.object: JTAG unlock/authentication state: jtag_unlock_o, pass_check, pass_mode, hashValid
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Unlock should require a completed HMAC/hash comparison against jtag_hash_i; bypass controls such as key_hash_bypass_i, pass_mode, we_flag, testmode_i, or reset should not force unlock or accept stale/partial hash state without authorization.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmi_jtag assigns jtag_unlock_o directly to pass_check. pass_check is a combinational signal defaulted to 0 and set only when hashValid is true and exp_hash equals pass_hash in the PassChkValid path. HMAC provides ready/hash_valid outputs and supports key_hash_bypass_i. The DMI read transition can be allowed by `~we_flag` independent of pass_check, and no sticky authenticated unlock register was observed.
evidence_slots.path: TAP data/address/op -> dmi_jtag pass_data/startHash -> hmac -> pass_hash/hashValid -> pass_check/jtag_unlock_o -> DMI request state machine
evidence_slots.state_condition: dmi_jtag reset clears state/address/data/error but not a separate persistent unlock register. hmac reset clears state/hash_o/ready_o/hash_valid_o. key_hash_bypass_i exists in hmac and dmi_jtag connects okey/ikey hashes from fuse_mem; exact bypass constant/control in dmi_jtag is partly truncated in observations.
evidence_slots.impact: Debug unlock semantics are weakened: unlock is a pulse-like combinational result, while DMI reads may bypass it through we_flag state.

rtl_evidence.detail: Authentication-related states/signals declared: PassChk, PassChkWait, PassChkValid, pass_check, pass_mode, startHash, exp_hash/pass_hash, pass_data, hmac_ready, hashValid.
jtag_unlock_o assigned to pass_check; exp_hash assigned from jtag_hash_i.

key_hash_bypass_i affects SHA init and hash-block update.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: JTAG unlock is not a persistent authenticated state and DMI read access can be permitted by we_flag bypass rather than a completed HMAC comparison.

evidence_slots.object: system memory/peripheral fabric reachable by debug master
evidence_slots.expected_guard: If debug master access to system memory/peripherals is supported, it should be gated by debug authentication/unlock and address/privilege policy; if unsupported, tie-offs must prevent any reachable transaction or stale response exposure.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: The debug module master/system-bus response path is tied off: dm_master_gnt, dm_master_r_valid, and dm_master_r_rdata are assigned zero, and comments state system bus accesses are not supported. No routing from dm_master_req/add/we/wdata/be to a NoC/AXI fabric was observed in the provided top-level snippet. This is distinct from the debug slave NoC port, which is covered by PERM_RTL_003.
evidence_slots.path: dm_top debug master signals -> top-level tie-offs/no system bus route
evidence_slots.state_condition: Tie-offs are constant assignments and not dependent on reset/debug_mode in observed code.
evidence_slots.impact: Debug-initiated system memory/peripheral master transactions appear disabled by construction in the provided evidence.

rtl_evidence.detail: dm_master request/address/write/data/response signals declared.
Comment says system bus accesses not supported; dm_master_gnt, dm_master_r_valid, dm_master_r_rdata are tied to zero.
Unused AXI-lite dm_master fields tied off.

inspection_records.notes: The debug slave register path remains a separate candidate issue under PERM_RTL_003.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible NoC/MMIO requester
evidence_slots.object: reset controls rst_1..rst_13 and affected security states
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Software-triggered resets of security-sensitive peripherals or debug/auth state should require privilege/access-control authorization and should not clear locks or authentication state in a way that grants access without reset-of-trust sequencing.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: The reset controller is exposed via a dedicated NoC AXI-lite bridge to rst_wrapper. rst_wrapper receives reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i derived from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12], and debug_mode_i, and outputs rst_1..rst_11 and rst_13. However, rst_wrapper internals, reset register update semantics, and mapping from rst_* to security-sensitive state were not provided, so authorization and side effects cannot be confirmed.
evidence_slots.path: reset NoC port -> i_rst_axilite_bridge -> rst_axi_req -> rst_wrapper -> rst_1..rst_13
evidence_slots.state_condition: Top-level system reset rst_ni is passed to rst_wrapper; software-controlled reset defaults and whether resets clear reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl/jtag_unlock/pass_check are not visible.
evidence_slots.impact: If reset controls are insufficiently guarded or clear locks/access policy/authentication state unsafely, software could bypass security sequencing.

rtl_evidence.detail: rst_1..rst_11 and rst_13 declared.
rst_wrapper receives reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i/debug_mode_i and outputs reset lines.
NoC AXI-lite bridge exposes reset wrapper request/response path.
JTAG TAP reset clears dmi_jtag DR/state/address/data/error; no persistent auth state observed.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: rst_wrapper internals, reset register permissions/defaults, rst_* mapping, and whether reset lines clear lock/access/authentication state.

evidence_slots.subject: DMA engine / DMA-configuring software
evidence_slots.object: DMA engine configuration and reachable address space
evidence_slots.expected_guard: DMA configuration and transfers should be restricted by access-control policy and address/range checks so DMA cannot access protected peripheral regions or bypass CPU privilege/PMP-equivalent restrictions.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: The DMA wrapper is reachable from a dedicated NoC AXI-lite bridge and receives reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i derived from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][8], pmpcfg_i, pmpaddr_i, we_flag_3, and rst_8. This indicates intended access and range mediation, but dma_wrapper internals and transfer address checks were not provided; therefore it cannot be confirmed whether both configuration writes and active transfers enforce PMP/range policy consistently.
evidence_slots.path: DMA NoC port -> i_dma_axilite_bridge -> dma_axi_req -> dma_wrapper -> DMA configuration/transfer logic
evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset input rst_8 is passed to dma_wrapper; behavior for active-transfer reconfiguration, descriptor residue, debug/test changes, and source/destination range checks is not visible.
evidence_slots.impact: If dma_wrapper does not enforce pmpcfg_i/pmpaddr_i and acct/reglk policy on configuration and transfers, DMA may bypass CPU privilege restrictions or access protected peripherals.

rtl_evidence.detail: dma_wrapper receives reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, pmpcfg_i, pmpaddr_i, we_flag_3, axi_req_i/axi_resp_o, and rst_8.
NoC AXI-lite bridge exposes DMA wrapper request/response path.
DMA AXI request fields not used by AXI-lite are tied off; no transfer-policy details visible.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: dma_wrapper internals for configuration write gating, active transfer controls, source/destination range checks, PMP enforcement, and reset behavior.
